Quick Facts

• Began swimming at age 7 when a family friend invited him to join a summer league team
• Was born in Kirovohrad, Ukraine, and moved to the United States at age 2 before settling in Minnesota at age 8
• Overcame balance challenges and the absence of stomach muscles through swimming
• Broke 30 seconds in the 50 free at age 8, celebrating with his brother Robin through a family tradition of passing a rubber chicken between them based on who had the faster time
• Draws inspiration from Paralympic legend Jessica Long, Olympic champion Michael Phelps, his parents and coach Jeff Rodriguez
• Away from the pool, enjoys video games, traveling, biking, exploring new places, and watching Big Brother and Survivor
• Loves Star Wars and lists the Bible among his favorite books
• Music playlist includes Lecrae and Morgan Wallen
• Owns a ball python named Noodles and has undergone 20 surgeries
• Family includes four siblings through adoption and foster care: Dre (24), Robin (16), Ryan (11), and Hannah (8)
• Goals include competing at the Paralympic Games, giving his best effort every race, and earning a spot on a Division I swim team

bio.education: Blessed Trinity High School (Roswell, Ga.) ‘11 University of Colorado - Colorado Springs
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • In October of 2003, Boyle acquired a traumatic brain injury. While riding a Big Wheel at a friend’s birthday party, he was hit and dragged by a pick-up truck causing him to immediately go into a coma. Emergency brain surgery was performed to save Boyle’s life, but he lost a portion of the back of his brain. At the age of 10, he had to learn to how to breath, swallow, talk, eat, stand, sit, walk – everything – all over again. Boyle wrote his own book titled, “When the Lights go Out: A Boy Given a Second Chance,” when he was only a freshman in high school. He is a resident athlete at the Olympic Training Center in Colorado Springs
  • Son of Matthew and Nancy Boyle
  • Has one brother, Matthew
  • Founding president of the St. Joseph High School Car Club and he also managed the boys’ varsity basketball team, was elected to the student council, was involved with the video production club and was a student ambassador
  • Moved to Georgia in January 2011 for better therapeutic opportunities
  • Has spoken at Blessed Trinity High School, Clemson University and at Safe America.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, silver (T1-2 - Time Trial - Men), 4th (T1-2 - Road Race - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – gold (T2 - Road Race - Men), bronze (T2 - Time Trial - Men)
  • Years of participation: T2 - Road Race - Men 2014, 2015, 2017, 2018, 2019; T2 - Time Trial - Men 2014, 2015, 2017, 2018, 2019
  • Medals: 8 (3 gold, 2 silver, 3 bronze)
    • Gold – 2019 (T2 - Road Race - Men); 2018 (T2 - Road Race - Men, T2 - Time Trial - Men)
    • Silver – 2017 (T2 - Time Trial - Men); 2014 (T2 - Time Trial - Men)
    • Bronze – 2019 (T2 - Time Trial - Men); 2015 (T2 - Road Race - Men, T2 - Time Trial - Men)

  • Hopes to go to medical school and become an orthopedic surgeon

Quick Facts
  • Boyt took up swimming in 2005 at age nine with Team Vortex swimming team in Fort Collins, Colorado. She holds two Parapan American records, having made her senior international debut in 2008. She was named Colorado Swimming’s 2009 Disabled Swimmer of the Year. Boyt is a two-time Parapan American gold medalist, with two silvers and a bronze medal

  • Has one brother, Caiden
  • Hobbies include hiking, camping, reading, eating sushi and playing the ukulele
  • Has the same matching tattoo as her brother

  • Born in the same hospital as teammate Lindsay Grogan
  • Nickname is Ry Pie.

Most Notable

  • Started playing sitting volleyball in April 2011

Physical Disability: Left Below the knee amputation

  • Injury suffered November 2016 while in Iraq on military duty

Path to the Sitting Volleyball National Team:: "Sitting volleyball was a means of therapy when I was in recovery at San Antonio Medical Center in San Antonio, Texas." ... Came up through U.S. National Sitting Team A2 program.

Did You Know

  • Was an Apache helicopter mechanic in the Army before starting to play sitting volleyball
  • Now retired from the Army and spends his days at the lake fishing. "I go fishing eight days a week."
  • Was born in Frankfurt, Germany

Happiest Moment in Life: "The birth of my two boys (Beckham and Cardin)."

  • Actor: Jim Carrey

Most Admired Person: "John Wayne (The Duke) because you darn know why!"

Hobbies: fishing, hunting, video games

Personal: Parents are Kevin Bracken and Shelly Glidden ... Sisters are Megan Bracken and Amelia Glidden ... Childen ae Beckham Bracken and Cardin Bracken.

Quick Facts
  • Graduated from Minnesota with a degree in public relations/advertising
  • Spent a season as an assistant coach at Cal-State Fullerton. She began playing beach volleyball competitively in 2004
  • Nicole married her wife Jenn in 2013. The couple has two children, daughter Tegan (2013) and son Will (2016)
Career Highlights
  • Tied for fifth at the 2008 Beijing Olympic Games with Elaine Youngs, falling in the semifinals to the eventual bronze medalists from China
  • Member of the Women's National Team from 2001-03 and played for the Pan Am Games bronze medal in 2003
  • Nicole's career kill total at Minnesota was sixth in NCAA DI history, yet she played in fewer games than any of the top five
  • ---
  • DID YOU KNOW
  • Has partnered with six Olympians through her career
  • Returned to the FIVB World Tour in 2017 to partner with Kerri Walsh Jennings, their best finish being fourth in Olsztyn, Poland; played the FIVB World Championships with Emily Day
  • In 2015, won the 2015 AVP Chicago Open with Jennifer Kropp, her first AVP win since 2010
  • On the FIVB World Tour between 2011-15, best finish was fourth in 2012 with Lauren Fendrick in Thailand
  • Teamed with Angie Akers and competed in 14 FIVB World Tour events in 2011. Their best finishes were fifth places in Sanya, China and Stare Jablonki, Poland
  • In the final 2010 FIVB World Tour stop of the season, Nicole partnered with Kerri Walsh to win the Phuket Thailand Open. Nicole also partnered with Misty May-Treanor for nine FIVB World Tour events, finishing third in Stavanger, Norway and fourth and fifth place twice, respectively
  • With Elaine Youngs in 2009, finished was second in Klagenfurt, Austria, third in Gstaad, Switzerland and fourth in Brazilia, Brazil. Duo won nine AVP tournaments in 2009, including the Manhattan Open
  • Following the 2008 Olympic Games, Nicole partnered with fellow Olympian Kerri Walsh to win the FIVB beach tour event in Dubai. The pair went 6-0 and did not lose a set
  • Nicole and partner Elaine Youngs, who were ranked fifth, finished tied for fifth at the 2008 Olympic Games, falling in the quarterfinals (17-21, 13-21) to the fourth-ranked Chinese team of Xue Chen and Zhang Xi, which went on to win the bronze medal
  • Nicole and Elaine clinched the second U.S. women's beach volleyball Olympic spot at the FIVB World Tour Grand Slam event in Moscow on July 5. Although Branagh and Youngs won the silver medal, it actually secured enough points for the slot with a 21-17, 21-14 semifinal win over fourth-seeded Brazilians Talita Antunes and Renata Ribeiro
  • Nicole and Elaine played in eight FIVB events in 2008 prior to the Olympic Games and won one gold medal (Barcelona), one silver (Moscow) and two bronze (Seoul and Paris)
  • Between 2006-07 on the FIVB World Tour, Branagh finished with a silver and two bronze as best finishes, all with Elaine Youngs
  • At Minnesota, was a two-year Second Team All-American and Big 10 Player of the Year in 2000
  • First Team All-Conference her sophomore-senior years, honorable mention her freshman season

Quick Facts
Son of Warren and Claire Braun, Braun was born with spinal bifida. A native of Jamaica, Braun was abandoned in a trash bin as an infant and eventually adopted by a missionary from Minnesota after being in an orphanage. He was introduced to adaptive sports through the Courage Kenny Rehabilitation Institute in Minnesota. He was the first athlete to compete in wheelchair track and field after the Minnesota High School league expanded to allow wheelchair athletes. In his free time, Braun enjoys basketball, golfing, music and playing sports. He also volunteers for a local recreational adaptive sports program. In 2015, Braun represented the Western All-Star Team as a participant in the 2015 NBA/NWBA All-Star Game in Brooklyn New York and was honored by the Minnesota Timberwolves. In the future, Braun hopes to become a coach of other athletes. He has seven siblings: Jennifer, Lucas, Meri, Michael, Penni, Sabrina and Victoria.
